title: GET /api/projects never returns the first 10 records — page=1 starts at proj_011
body:
### Bug report

Our nightly sync pulls all projects through the paginated list endpoint and we noticed records are missing on our side. Repro against production:

```bash
$ curl -s "https://api.acme.dev/api/projects?page=1&page_size=10" | jq '.items[].id'
"proj_011"
"proj_012"
...
"proj_020"

$ curl -s "https://api.acme.dev/api/projects?page=2&page_size=10" | jq '.items[].id'
"proj_021"
...
"proj_025"
```

The response says `"total": 25`, but paging through every page only ever yields 15 distinct records — `proj_001` through `proj_010` are never returned on any page or page size we tried. Docs say pages are 1-indexed, so page=1 should start at the first record. `page=0` does return the first ten, but the validation clamps it back to 1 on some routes so we can't rely on that.

Our integration is currently missing 10 projects for every customer we sync.

title: Pagination: page=2 with page_size=10 returns only 5 items when total is 25
body:
### Bug report

Expected page 2 to contain items 11–20 of our 25 projects, but the API returns the last 5 instead and `hasMore` is already false:

```json
{
  "items": ["proj_021", "proj_022", "proj_023", "proj_024", "proj_025"],
  "page": 2,
  "pageSize": 10,
  "total": 25,
  "hasMore": false
}
```

(ids elided from the item objects for brevity)

Looks like an off-by-one-page somewhere — everything we get is shifted one full page forward. Breaks our paging UI, which shows empty pages at the end and never shows the earliest projects.
